fields.summary: Add security check for container user is same as websocket user
[Umbrella] Support Interactive Docker Shell to running Containers

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: When user is authenticate via SPNEGO entry point, node manager must verify the remote user is the same as the container user to start the web socket session.  One possible solution is to verify the web request user matches yarn container local directory owne during onWebSocketConnect..

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Enhance ACL to set a list of users to access container shell

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

comments.body: Patch 002 allows YARN admin user to login to container, if yarn.acl.enable feature is enabled.
Thanks for the patch, [~eyang]. It looks like this is not performing any authorization check in the nonsecure case. Could we check the container user against the provided user.name?
[~billie.rinaldi] Thank you for the review.  Patch 005 added user.name check for non-secure cluster.
Thanks for the patch, [~eyang]! I tested out patch 6 and it looks good. +1, pending precommit build.

comments.body: Patch 002 allows YARN admin user to login to container, if yarn.acl.enable feature is enabled.
Thanks for the patch, [~eyang]. It looks like this is not performing any authorization check in the nonsecure case. Could we check the container user against the provided user.name?
[~billie.rinaldi] Thank you for the review.  Patch 005 added user.name check for non-secure cluster.
Thanks for the patch, [~eyang]! I tested out patch 6 and it looks good. +1, pending precommit build.
